services.name: Google Gemini API
services.free_tier: 2.5 Flash & Flash-Lite: 1,500 req/day (15–30 RPM), 1M TPM. 2.5 Pro: 50 req/day. 2.0 Flash: 1,500 req/day.
services.free_tier_notes: Google no longer publishes a static free-tier table; figures via third-party tracker. Quotas have changed without notice before.
services.best_for: Best free daily quota for frontier-class multimodal models

services.name: OpenAI API
services.free_tier: No standing free quota. Occasional small one-time trial credits for new accounts (not documented officially).
services.free_tier_notes: Confirm current promo credit status at signup.
services.paid_price:
gpt-5.4-nano: $0.10/M in, $0.625/M out
gpt-5.6-sol (flagship): $2.50/M in, $15/M out
services.best_for: Broadest ecosystem/tooling support; no meaningful free tier

services.name: Anthropic Claude API
services.free_tier: Small one-time free credits for new API accounts (amount unpublished); no recurring free quota.
services.free_tier_notes: Batch API is 50% off; prompt-cache reads are 0.1x input price.
services.paid_price:
Haiku 4.5: $1/M in, $5/M out
Sonnet 5: $2/M in, $10/M out (promo to Aug 31, 2026; then $3/$15)
Opus 4.8: $5/M in, $25/M out
services.best_for: Strong coding/agentic performance; Sonnet promo pricing until Aug 2026

services.name: Mistral API (La Plateforme)
services.free_tier: Free tier exists (~1 req/sec, 500K tokens/min per third-party tracker); exact limits shown per-account in dashboard.
services.free_tier_notes: Not confirmed on official page this run — check admin.mistral.ai/plateforme/limits for your account.
services.paid_price:
Mistral Large: $2/M in, $6/M out
Mistral Small: sub-$1/M range
services.best_for: European-hosted alternative with a usable no-card free tier

services.name: Groq
services.free_tier: ~30 RPM for most models; 100–14,400 req/day depending on model (llama-3.1-8b-instant: 14.4K/day); ~6K–15K TPM.
services.free_tier_notes: Limits vary per model; check your account limits page for exact values.
services.paid_price:
Llama 3.1 8B Instant: $0.05/M in, $0.08/M out
GPT-OSS-20B: $0.075/M in, $0.30/M out
Llama 4 Scout: $0.11/M in, $0.34/M out
services.best_for: Fastest inference (100s–1000+ tok/s) on open models; great free tier for prototyping

services.name: Cohere
services.free_tier: Trial key: 1,000 API calls/month total; Chat capped at 20 req/min; separate per-minute caps per endpoint.
services.free_tier_notes: The 1,000-call monthly ceiling is the binding constraint — evaluation only.
services.paid_price: ~$2.50/M in, $10/M out (Command R+ class, third-party cited)
services.best_for: Enterprise RAG/rerank; free tier good for evaluation only

services.name: OpenRouter
services.free_tier: 25+ rotating free models across providers; per-model rate limits vary.
services.free_tier_notes: Free-model lineup fluctuates — see openrouter.ai/openrouter/free for the current list.
services.paid_price: No markup on provider token prices; 5.5% platform fee on credit purchases
services.best_for: One API for many providers, including rotating free models

services.name: DeepSeek API
services.free_tier: No free tier or free credits.
services.free_tier_notes: Legacy deepseek-chat/reasoner names deprecate Jul 24, 2026 → folded into deepseek-v4-flash.
services.paid_price:
V4-Flash: $0.14/M in (cache miss), $0.28/M out
V4-Pro: $0.435/M in, $0.87/M out
Cache hits ~50x cheaper on input
services.best_for: Very cheap frontier-adjacent reasoning, especially with cache hits

services.name: Together AI
services.free_tier: None. No free trials; minimum $5 prepaid credit purchase required.
services.free_tier_notes: Separate startup credit programs may exist outside the standard signup flow.
services.paid_price:
gpt-oss-20B: $0.05/M in, $0.20/M out
Gemma 3n E4B: $0.06/M in, $0.12/M out
services.best_for: Wide open-model catalog at competitive per-token prices; no free tier

services.name: Hugging Face Inference Providers
services.free_tier: Free accounts: $0.10/month in routed inference credits (pass-through pricing). PRO: $2.00/month included.
services.free_tier_notes: HF explicitly notes the free credit amount is subject to change.
services.paid_price: Pass-through pricing from underlying providers; no HF markup
services.best_for: Huge model catalog via one API; free allowance is minimal

services.name: GitHub Actions
services.free_tier: Public repos: unlimited free minutes. Private repos (Free plan): 2,000 min/month.
services.free_tier_notes: Private-repo minutes have OS multipliers (Linux 1x, Windows 2x, macOS ~10x).
services.paid_price: Overage billed per minute beyond quota; rate depends on runner OS
services.best_for: Free cron/CI engine — effectively unlimited on public repos

services.name: Cloudflare Workers + Pages
services.free_tier: Workers: 100K req/day, 10ms CPU/request. Pages: 500 builds/month, no published cap on static bandwidth.
services.free_tier_notes: Exceeding Workers daily cap returns error 1027. Pages: 25 MiB/asset, 20K files/site.
services.best_for: Generous free static bandwidth (Pages) + real serverless free tier (Workers)

services.name: Vercel (Hobby)
services.free_tier: 1M edge requests, 1M function invocations, 4 active CPU-hrs, 100 deploys/day, up to 200 projects.
services.free_tier_notes: Hobby plan is non-commercial use only; overages pause features (~30 days) rather than auto-charge.
services.best_for: Personal Next.js/frontend projects with generous request quota

services.name: Netlify
services.free_tier: 300 credits/month pool: 15 credits per production deploy, 20/GB bandwidth, 2 per 10K requests.
services.free_tier_notes: Replaced the old flat '100GB bandwidth / 300 build minutes' free tier with credit accounting.
services.paid_price: Paid plans from ~$19/month with larger credit allotments
services.best_for: Static/Jamstack hosting with simple credit accounting

services.name: Supabase
services.free_tier: 500MB Postgres (shared CPU, 500MB RAM), 5GB egress/month, up to 2 active projects.
services.free_tier_notes: Free projects auto-pause after 1 week of inactivity — the classic side-project gotcha.
services.best_for: Postgres backend-as-a-service for prototypes (mind the auto-pause)
